stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 5 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 15 observations with mean 54.1 points (within-school SD = 9.6). school B has 15 observations with mean 61.7 points (within-school SD = 8.7). school C has 8 observations with mean 60.4 points (within-school SD = 7.5). school D has 20 observations with mean 57.4 points (within-school SD = 9.1). school E has 20 observations with mean 47.0 points (within-school SD = 7.9). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.4 and 8.4 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school C: 59.4 points (shrunk from raw 60.4). Range [58.4, 59.9] as between-school SD varies from 3.4 to 8.4. Pooled mean: 55.2.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 55.2 points (weighted average of 5 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=5.6: B = 0.817.
Interval [58.4, 59.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (60.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (55.2) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
